pr.title: Fix some embedded structs in Fighter
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Flattened several embedded bitfield helper structs inside struct Fighter into direct Fighter bitfields, then updated all call sites from forms like fp->x2220_flag.b3 and fp->x222A_flag.b0 to fp->x2220_b3 and fp->x222A_b0. The main affected offsets were fp+2220, fp+2229:5, and fp+222A. The change clarified that these are byte-level Fighter bitfields rather than meaningful nested substructures, preserved the Fighter size assert, and removed a few targets from tools/functions.sh's melee_sed file list. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice, so rationale beyond the title and diff is inferred from the code changes.
what_changed: In src/melee/ft/types.h, replaced the embedded struct x2220_flag with direct Fighter bitfields x2220_b0 through x2220_b7 at fp+2220; x2220_b0 remains a 3-bit field and x2220_b3 through x2220_b7 remain 1-bit fields.
Renamed the fp+2229:5 bitfield from x2229_b5_no_normal_motion to no_normal_motion, matching the existing OSReport string "ellegal flag fp->no_normal_motion" in Fighter_Create.
Replaced the generic UnkFlagStruct x222A_flag at fp+222A with explicit bitfields x222A_b0, x222A_b1, x222A_b2, x222A_b3:2, x222A_b5, x222A_b6, and x222A_b7.
Updated Fighter initialization, motion state, mushroom state, camera/draw, collision, and boss on-load code to use the flattened field names.
In tools/functions.sh, removed asm, obj_files.mk, and Makefile from the file list traversed by melee_sed.
smart_moves: Flattening the bitfields removed artificial embedded structs while retaining exact offset-and-bit comments such as fp+2220:3 and fp+222A:0, making later decomp work easier to audit against memory layout.
The PR updated all call sites in the same patch, avoiding a mixed style where some code used x2220_flag.bN while other code used x2220_bN.
Keeping a Fighter size static assert at 0x23EC guarded against layout drift while changing bitfield declarations.
The x222A_b3 field was declared as a 2-bit field, preserving the apparent bit packing from bit 3 through bit 4 before x222A_b5.
The meaningful name no_normal_motion was promoted from a suffix in x2229_b5_no_normal_motion to the field name itself, reducing noisy offset-prefixed naming where a behavior was already known.
decomp_lessons: Do not introduce nested flag structs merely because several adjacent bits share a byte; if the original code treats them as independent Fighter flags, direct bitfields with offset comments can be clearer and less misleading.
When flattening bitfields, update every user in fighter, character-specific, and item-interaction code so references remain grepable by the canonical field name.
For byte-sized unknown flag groups, explicit bit widths are important: x2220_b0 is 3 bits, x222A_b3 is 2 bits, and the remaining fields are 1 bit.
A named semantic bit such as no_normal_motion should be preferred when there is direct evidence from existing strings or behavior.
After struct layout edits, retain or strengthen static assertions so matching work can catch accidental size changes immediately.
naming_conventions: Flattened Fighter bitfields use the pattern xOFFSET_bBIT, for example x2220_b3, x2220_b4, x2220_b5, x2220_b6, x2220_b7, x222A_b0, and x222A_b1.
Known semantic flags can drop the offset prefix, as shown by x2229_b5_no_normal_motion becoming no_normal_motion.
Avoid names like x2220_flag.b3 or x222A_flag.b0 when the embedded struct name does not encode a real domain concept.
Offset comments retain the fp+OFFSET:BIT notation even when the C field name is semantic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Flattening anonymous or helper bitfield structs can improve C source readability without necessarily changing generated code, provided bit widths and ordering are preserved.
Use the existing struct size assert, here sizeof(struct Fighter) == 0x23EC, as a quick guardrail after bitfield packing changes.
When replacing a generic UnkFlagStruct with explicit fields, account for multi-bit spans such as x222A_b3 : 2 instead of assuming every label is one bit.
searchable_terms: Fix some embedded structs in Fighter
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that assign semantic names to x2220_b3, x2220_b4, x2220_b5, x2220_b6, x2220_b7, x222A_b0, or x222A_b1.
Search for remaining embedded flag structs in Fighter or other core structs that may be artificial layout artifacts.
Check whether removal of asm, obj_files.mk, and Makefile from melee_sed was part of a broader tooling convention for safe project-wide substitutions.
